pattern: Check each word against every other word as a substring; short-circuit once a match is found to avoid redundant work.
hints: A word qualifies only if it appears as a proper substring of some other word in the array.
For each word w, scan all other words and check whether w is contained in them using the 'in' operator or indexOf.
Sort by length first so you only compare shorter words against longer ones—but simple O(n^2 * L) is well within bounds here.

edge_inputs.description: example 2: two substrings of one long word
no word is a substring of any other
single word, trivially no substring word
all words identical—none is a 'proper' substring of another (same length)
a word is a prefix but not a proper infix of another
word is both prefix and suffix of another

stress.description: 100 words of length 1-30 with some guaranteed substrings
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    n = min(size, 100)
    chars = string.ascii_lowercase
    words = list({''.join(rng.choice(chars) for _ in range(rng.randint(1, 15))) for _ in range(n * 2)})[:n]
    # Embed some substrings
    for i in range(min(5, len(words))):
        long_word = words[i]
        if len(long_word) >= 3:
            sub = long_word[1:-1]
            if sub not in words:
                words.append(sub)
    words = list(set(words))[:n]
    return [words]
